subject: Re: Problem with template matching in warp
body:
Hi Delnia,

Are your tilt series alignments good? Did you try playing with the contrast
when opening the template matching volumes to inspect the peaks?

Cheers,

Alister

On Sun, 6 Jul 2025 at 04:54, Deli <delnianazari@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hello all,
>
> I'm trying to do warp template matching for ribosome in human cells on my
> linux desktop and the result looks kinda weird for me.
> The command and parameters I'm using is:
>
> WarpTools ts_template_match \
>
> -﻿- settings warp_tiltseries.settings
>
> ﻿-- tomo_directory warp_tiltseries/reconstruction \
>
> -- ﻿output_processing /warptemplatematching_output \
>
> --tomo_angpix 15.44 \ (my tomograms are in bin 8)
>
> -﻿- template_emdb 44909 \
>
> -- template diameter 300 \
>
> -﻿-perdevice 2 \
>
> ﻿-- template_angpix 15.44 \
>
> -- ﻿subdivisions 2 \
>
> --npeak 300 \
>
> --check_hand 4 \
>
> (I also tried to play around with parameters but the output contains the
> same problem.)
>
> The log file would be:
>
> Running command ts_template_match with:
>
> tomo_angpix = 15.44
>
> tomo_directory = warp_tiltseries/reconstruction
>
> template_path = null
>
> template_emdb = 44909
>
> template_angpix = 15.44
>
> template_diameter = 300
>
> template_flip = False
>
> symmetry = C1
>
> subdivisions = 2
>
> tilt_range = null
>
> batch_angles = 32
>
> peak_distance = null
>
> npeaks = 300
>
> dont_normalize = False
>
> whiten = False
>
> lowpass = 1
>
> lowpass_sigma = 0.1
>
> max_missing_tilts = 2
>
> reuse_results = False
>
> check_hand = 4
>
> subvolume_size = 192
>
> device_list = {  }
>
> perdevice = 2
>
> workers = {  }
>
> settings = warp_tiltseries.settings
>
> input_data = {  }
>
> input_data_recursive = False
>
> input_processing = null
>
> output_processing = /warptemplatematching_output
>
> input_norawdata = False
>
>
> No alternative input specified, will use input parameters from
> warp_tiltseries.s
>
> ettings
>
> File search will be relative to /20250516_bjeh/tomostar
>
> 102 files found
>
> Parsing previous results for each item, if available...
>
> 0/102
>
>
> Both --input_processing and --output_processing are set, attempting to
> link tomo
>
> grams from /20250516_bjeh/warptemplatematching_output/reconstruction...
>
> Reconstruction directory successfully linked.
>
>
>
>
> Extracting downloaded map... Done
>
> Preparing flipped template... Done
>
> Using 4608 orientations for matching
>
> Connecting to workers...
>
> Connected to 8 workers
>
> Testing matching with original template:
>
> 0/4
>
> Finished processing in 00:05:21
>
> Average top peak value with original template: 2.059
>
> Testing matching with flipped template:
>
> 0/4
>
> Finished processing in 00:05:20
>
> Average top peak value with flipped template: 2.057

> Original template has higher peak values, using it for further processing
>

>
> Saying goodbye to all workers... Done
>
>
>
> and the matching results looks like the attached image.
>
>
> Is there any suggestion to solve the problem?
>
>
> Best,
>
> Delnia
